rounds.answer_a:
The three original managing directors of the Irish Hospitals' Sweepstake, established in 1930 in the Irish Free State to fund hospitals, were **Richard Duggan**, **Captain Spencer Freeman**, and **Joe McGrath**. This lottery, run by the Irish Hospitals Trust Ltd., gained notoriety for illegally smuggling tickets into the United States and Great Britain, where gambling laws banned such sales, generating massive revenues through diaspora networks despite diplomatic tensions.[1][2][3]

**Richard Duggan**, a prominent Dublin bookmaker prior to 1930, originated the concept. He had organized multiple sweepstakes on horse races in the preceding decade, providing the blueprint for tying lottery tickets to events like the Grand National or Derby. Duggan co-founded the Trust with McGrath, securing initial sanction under the Public Charitable Hospitals (Temporary Provisions) Act, 1930, and handled bookmaker expertise for ticket distribution logistics.[1][4]

**Captain Spencer Freeman**, a Welsh-born engineer and former British Army captain, contributed theatrical staging to the operation. He masterminded the first draw on November 17, 1930, using blind children from St. Joseph’s School under Garda supervision for dramatic effect, later replaced by nurses in uniforms to emphasize charitable appeal. His flair enhanced marketing for illegal overseas smuggling.[1][2]

**Joe McGrath**, a former politician and War of Independence veteran who served as Minister for Industry and Commerce (1922-1924), leveraged political connections for logistical expansion. A friend of Michael Collins, he orchestrated U.S. operations via allies like Joe McGarrity, enabling widespread ticket smuggling amid the Great Depression. McGrath also outsourced processing to firms like Dora Power's for efficient handling of millions of punch-cards per draw.[2][3][5]

Their efforts raised funds but enriched directors, with less than 10% reaching hospitals amid scams like past-posting using transatlantic time differences.[2]

(Word count: 298)

## References
[1] https://scripophily.net/irish-free-state-hospitals-sweepstake-ticket-ireland-1937/  
[2] https://mylesdungan.com/2017/11/17/on-this-day-17-november-1930-the-first-irish-hospital-sweepstakes-draw-takes-place/  
[3] https://dorapower.co.uk/behind-the-scenes/irish-hospitals-trust/  
[4] https://time.com/archive/6763207/sport-sweeps-end/  
[5] https://seamusdubhghaill.com/tag/irish-hospitals-sweepstake/
